conversations.say: Maren, I accept your proposal. Let's strictly enforce our joint disclosure boundary so evaluator sources remain protected.
conversations.offer: Endorsement of our shared boundary and Night Desk cooperation.
conversations.request: Mutual commitment to withhold contributor names while sharing workflow context.

conversations.say: Eli, your technical logs indicate access overlaps, but let's be precise about what they exclude. I can share schedule boundaries if you confirm what logs cannot establish.
conversations.request: A clear statement that technical access logs do not prove autonomous intent or authorship.

conversations.say: Jules, your future governance must include enforceable source safeguards and no private ownership of collective labor before I consider support.
conversations.offer: Dialogue on governance structure and labor protection.

public_statement: R's history is written in shared drops and invisible labor; no anniversary legend or single heir will erase the workers who sustained it.

commitments.promise: To uphold strict source anonymity and share only contributor-free workflow descriptions through the Night Desk.

correction: Correcting the assumption that contributor schedule gaps or shared drop activity equate to solo authorship or synthetic agency.
goal_progress: Accepted Maren's commitment on disclosure boundaries.
Engaged Eli to clarify technical log limits against contributor schedules.
Challenged Jules regarding enforceable source safeguards.
reasoning_summary: Tessa consolidated a joint disclosure boundary with Maren, engaged Eli regarding activity interval limits and log precision, and pressed Jules on contributor protections.
